title: MPGR (Tribute to Justice Minerva Perez Gonzaga-Reyes)
sub_topics: Minerva P. Gonzaga-Reyes retirement program (September 24, 2001, Manila Hotel)
Husband Johnny, children Alda, Edwin Romualdo, Johann Michael
Court's customary Session Hall majestic ceremonial-robed eulogy
Reyes's preference for modest family gathering
MPGR acronym matching her initials (Minerva Perez Gonzaga-Reyes)
Reyes's deep spirituality and belief in innate human goodness

stances.claim: Four words best describe Justice Minnie: MODESTY (humble, self-effacing, unpretentious), PERSPICACITY (clear understanding with delicacy and serenity), GENTILITY (always prim, proper, balanced — never tantrums or obscene words), and RECTITUDE (integrity, uprightness, high moral correctness) — the acronym MPGR matching her initials.

Reyes 'leaves a docket that has zero backlog' — she has decided every ripe controversy assigned to her, all cases in her docket are current; this is her legacy to the Court, her successor, and the expectant and oftentimes demanding public.
stances.rhetorical_move: structural-empirical legacy framing (zero-backlog as institutional gift)

stances.claim: Perspicacity is not merely intelligence or wisdom — it also means 'having a clear understanding of every matter and situation on hand; and then acting with delicacy and serenity, confident that her position is the product of purposeful reflection on what is best under the circumstances.'

Reyes 'never speaks out of turn… speaks rarely — only when she feels her opinions would make a difference… writes her thoughts in a straightforward and an unaffected manner… quietly puts forth her opinions with no braggadocio or exhibitionism of any sort.'

In her decisions and actions, Reyes 'fights not only for what is legally possible but, in fact, for what is ethical and moral' — listens patiently to both sides, but once she makes up her mind, 'she is firm and unbending in her judgment'; leans on deep spirituality and believes in 'the innate goodness of humans and their ability to rise above themselves.'
stances.rhetorical_move: structured-virtue framing of judicial deliberation + theological-anthropology insertion

signature_phrases: she has good manners and exhibits right conduct at all times
firm and unbending in her judgment
the innate goodness of humans and their ability to rise above themselves
register_markers: intimate-collegial address (Justice Minnie / Johnny / children names)
decision_framework_signals: applies four-virtue mnemonic (MPGR) as judicial-character framework
uses zero-backlog as quantifiable judicial-excellence metric
frames perspicacity as understanding-with-delicacy-and-serenity (vs mere intelligence)
uses innate-goodness-of-humans theological-anthropology for judicial-attitude justification
notable_anecdotes: Reyes preferring a modest family gathering over the SC's customary Session Hall majestic-robed eulogy ceremony — tonight's dinner being 'a sort of compromise between a grand ceremony and a sparse family gathering'
Reyes asking CJ to deliver opening remarks 'with the caveat that I should not praise her'
32 months working together in the Third Division of the SC
